'''Ung Kusp''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] who commanded the loyalty of a Mandalorian [[Unidentified Mandalorian clan (Ung Kusp)|clan]] many centuries before the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].

==Biography==
A post-[[Mandalorian Wars]] [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]], Ung Kusp followed their agenda of the pursuit of [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] with no regard for justice, morality, or honor.<ref name="HOTM">{{InsiderCite|80|The History of the Mandalorians: Exploring the Evolution of a Mighty Race of Warriors}}</ref> Kusp's lack of honor alienated Master [[Jaing]],<ref name="Prot" /> a former [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusader]] faithful to the code of the [[Canons of Honor]], who took Kusp as his chief rival.<ref name="CSWEJ">{{CSWECite|II|135|Jaing, Master}}</ref> Leaving Ung Kusp's [[Unidentified Mandalorian clan (Ung Kusp)|clan]], those who remained loyal to Kusp accused Jaing of betraying the mercenary, while Jaing maintained that it was Kusp who had betrayed him.<ref name="Prot" />

Many centuries before the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]],<ref name="CSWEN">{{CSWECite|II|394|Nubyl}}</ref> Ung Kusp's clan was hired to recover a precious [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Emperor's cane|artifact]] stolen by a [[Unidentified Outer Rim cyberneticist|cyberneticist]], sending a force of mercenaries to the cyberneticist's facility in a an [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] industrial wasteland. Breaching facility's door, Kusp's Mandalorians were intercepted by Jaing, his protegé Durge, and [[Nubyl]], all [[Cybernetics/Legends|cybernetically]] enhanced by their target. Many Mandalorians were felled by the defenders, but the three were eventually dispatched by the explosion of a [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonator]]. Recovering the artifact, a [[Unidentified Mandalorian (Ung Kusp)|Mandalorian]] mocked an injured Jaing for his honor and threatened to turn his head into a trophy for Kusp's wall, only to be taken by surprise by Jaing, the two inflicting mortal wounds on each other. Jaing's death motivated Durge to massacre Ung Kusp's force and leave for [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]], swearing vengeance against the Mandalorians. In reality, the attack had been orchestrated by the cyberneticist in a plot to ignite a war between the Mandalorians and Sith.<ref name="Prot" />
